title: 10 best herbal capsules for angina
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for angina are natural supplements that combine traditional herbs with modern manufacturing techniques to provide a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments. These capsules contain ingredients such as garlic, Hawthorne berries, and Ginkgo biloba, which help to improve blood flow, lower cholesterol levels, and reduce inflammation, ultimately reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks. For example, capsules containing Ginkgo biloba have been shown to increase oxygen supply to the heart, while those with Hawthorne berries have been found to dilate blood vessels, allowing for better circulation. By taking these herbal capsules, individuals with angina can experience improved quality of life, reduced symptoms, and increased confidence in their daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with angina because they contain a unique combination of compounds that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. The capsules' active ingredients, including flavonoids and saponins, work synergistically to dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle and reducing symptoms of angina. Additionally, these compounds have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, thereby reducing the formation of blood clots that can contribute to anginal episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: These properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of cardiovascular diseases like angina.

Vasodilation and blood flow enhancement: Ginkgo biloba extract has been shown to improve blood flow and increase nitric oxide production, which can help alleviate symptoms of angina by reducing blood vessel constriction and improving oxygen delivery to the heart.

Cardioprotective effects: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba extracts may have cardioprotective properties, including reducing blood pressure, cholesterol levels, and platelet aggregation, all of which can contribute to a lower risk of developing angina.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps to improve blood flow and oxygen delivery to the heart, reducing symptoms of angina.
Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Act as antioxidants to protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to the reduction of angina symptoms.
Ginkgolides (including ginkgolide A and ginkgolide B): Have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of angina.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in capsule form, as they contain ginkgolides, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation.

Leaves (extracts): Ginkgo biloba leaf extracts are often used in capsules due to their high bioavailability and ability to improve blood flow, reducing symptoms of angina and other cardiovascular conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 20-30 dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 500mg of carrier material such as cornstarch or cellulose in a ratio of 1:10.
Fill a capsule machine with the powdered mixture and use it to fill 100-200 capsules in 10-15 minutes.
Label and package the capsules in airtight containers and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with angina because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that can help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation. This can reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks by increasing oxygen supply to the heart muscle. Additionally, turmeric's potent antioxidant properties can help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation, which is often associated with cardiovascular disease. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, turmeric capsules may provide natural relief from angina symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which may contribute to angina symptoms.

Antioxidant property: Curcumin also exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and protect against damage to blood vessels and cardiac tissues, potentially reducing the risk of angina attacks.
Vasodilatory property: Turmeric has been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart muscle and reducing symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to angina.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help protect the heart from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can exacerbate angina.

Turmerones: Turmerones, a group of sesquiterpenes, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which can contribute to angina.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes contain high concentrations of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate angina symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are a rich source of curcuminoids, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, helping to manage angina.
Barks: The barks of Curcuma longa contain various bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried curcuma longa roots with 1-2% curcumin content from a reputable supplier.
Grind 20 grams of the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 2 grams of starch and 2 grams of vegetable cellulose as fillers in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with angina because they contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and hyperoside, which help to dilate coronary arteries and increase blood flow to the heart. This increased blood flow allows for more oxygen-rich blood to reach the heart muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks. Additionally, hawthorn's bioactive compounds also help to strengthen the heart muscle itself, improving its ability to pump blood efficiently and effectively. As a result, hawthorn capsules can provide relief from angina symptoms and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn capsules contain cardiac glycosides, which help to improve blood flow to the heart muscle and reduce symptoms of angina by increasing the strength and efficiency of the heart's contractions.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in hawthorn capsules have antioxidant properties that help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to the development of angina.

Procyanidins: Procyanidins, a type of flavonoid found in hawthorn capsules, have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce peripheral resistance, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of angina by increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Crataegus monogyna, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, thereby alleviating angina symptoms.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as ursolic acid, in Crataegus monogyna have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which help to increase blood flow to the heart and reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks.

Glycosides: Glycosides, including crataegus glycosides, in Crataegus monogyna have been found to have a positive inotropic effect on the heart, which helps to increase cardiac output and reduce the workload on the heart, thereby alleviating angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds believed to have a beneficial effect on cardiovascular health.
Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.
Buds: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that may help to relax blood vessels and improve cardiovascular function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried hawthorn berries and 10g of dried leaves from the crataegus monogyna plant.
Dry and sift 100g of the dried hawthorn berries to ensure uniform size and quality.
Mix 100g of the sifted hawthorn berries with 10g of dried leaves in a large bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal according to manufacturer's instructions for 20 capsules.
Store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with angina because they contain active compounds that dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart. This increased blood flow reduces the frequency and severity of anginal attacks by providing more oxygen to the heart muscle. Additionally, garlic's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to its cardioprotective effects. By incorporating herbal garlic capsules into one's routine, individuals may experience a significant reduction in angina symptoms and improved overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal garlic capsules contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to angina symptoms.

Nitric oxide production: Garlic's active compound, allicin, helps increase nitric oxide production in the body, which relaxes blood vessels and improves blood flow to the heart, reducing anginal episodes.

Antithrombotic properties: Herbal garlic capsules have been shown to prevent blood clotting and reduce platelet aggregation, which can help alleviate angina symptoms by preventing blockages in coronary arteries.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: A sulfur compound that helps with angina by acting as a vasodilator, relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow to the heart, reducing the risk of angina attacks.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that helps with angina by reducing inflammation, improving blood lipid profiles, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which contribute to a reduced risk of angina.

S-allylcysteine (SAC): A sulfur-containing amino acid that helps with angina by reducing oxidative stress, improving blood flow, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which contribute to a reduced risk of angina.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that have cardiovascular benefits.
Leaves: They are used because they contain various compounds that help to prevent cardiovascular diseases.
Seeds: They are used due to their potential in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 1 kg of allium sativum bulbs and dry them at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried bulbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10% of the powder with 90% of a carrier like potato starch or tapioca powder.
Fill a capsule machine with 500 mg of the powder mixture and compress at 1000 pounds.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with angina because they contain natural compounds that have a profound impact on blood flow and circulation. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger help to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving oxygen delivery to the heart muscle. Additionally, ginger's ability to reduce inflammation in the coronary arteries can help to alleviate symptoms of angina by increasing blood flow to the heart and reducing the frequency and severity of attacks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina symptoms by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress.

Nitrative and vasodilatory effects: Ginger's active compounds, particularly 6-gingerol, have been shown to relax blood vessels, increase nitric oxide production, and improve blood flow, all of which help alleviate angina symptoms by decreasing blood pressure and increasing oxygen supply to the heart.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's high antioxidant capacity helps protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate angina symptoms by damaging the cardiovascular system and reducing its ability to function efficiently.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol, a phenolic compound, helps alleviate angina symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the heart.
Shogaol: Shogaol, another phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to relax blood vessels and improve coronary blood flow, reducing angina symptoms.
6-Gingerol: 6-Gingerol, a phenolic compound, has vasodilatory effects that help to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the severity of angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: The main underground stem of the Zingiber officinale plant, which contains the highest concentration of active compounds.
Roots: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used for their medicinal properties, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Stems: Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used in herbal supplements due to their potential cardiovascular benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried zingiber officinale roots in a clean environment for future use.
Grind 250 grams of the harvested root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground root powder with 10 grams of cellulose in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 0.5 grams of the root mixture using a capsule filler for 2 minutes.
Label and store the herbal zingiber officinale capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with angina because it has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. This natural supplement contains hyperforin, a compound that acts as a nitric oxide booster, which relaxes blood vessels and increases blood flow to the heart. As a result, herbal St John's wort capsules can help alleviate symptoms of angina, including chest pain and shortness of breath, by providing relief from reduced blood flow to the heart muscle.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in St John's Wort, such as hyperforin and flavonoids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to the development of angina.

Vasodilatory effects: The hypericins in St John's Wort capsules relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing the risk of anginal episodes by increasing oxygen delivery to the myocardium.

Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the coronary arteries, which can contribute to the development of angina.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound that helps with angina by inhibiting the reuptake of serotonin and other neurotransmitters, which can help reduce pain and improve mood.
Emodin: Emodin is a anthraquinone glycoside that helps with angina by relaxing blood vessels, improving blood flow, and reducing inflammation in the blood vessels.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that helps with angina by inhibiting platelet aggregation, improving blood flow, and reducing inflammation in the blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of hypericin and hyperforin, which have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate angina symptoms.
Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids, which contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce angina symptoms.
Seeds: They are used due to their high content of hypericin and other compounds that have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in summer months when they are in full bloom.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm place with air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Use a mill or coffee grinder to crush the dried plant material into a fine powder.
Mix 1 part of the hypericum perforatum powder with 2 parts of a binding agent like gelatin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with angina because they contain a blend of herbs that work together to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in these herbal teas help to dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen delivery to the heart and reducing symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath. Additionally, some herbs may also help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, further reducing the risk of angina episodes.
remedy_properties: Oxidative stress reduction: Herbal teas such as hawthorn, ginkgo biloba, and garlic capsules can reduce oxidative stress by scavenging free radicals, which helps to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the heart, reducing angina symptoms.

Vasodilation: Herbal teas like hawthorn, passionflower, and motherwort capsules contain compounds that help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which can alleviate angina episodes.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal teas such as turmeric, ginger, and willow bark capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which is a known risk factor for angina.
remedy_constituents: Catechin: Catechin has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing angina symptoms.
Theaflavin: Theaflavin has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.
Quercetin: Quercetin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of angina.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Camellia sinensis for making capsules due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate angina symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, making them a suitable component for angina capsules.
Stems: Stems are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the effectiveness of angina capsules in managing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of camellia sinensis leaves when they are young and fresh for optimal flavor and aroma.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours to remove moisture and preserve potency.
Crush 50g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1g of the powder with 1g of gelatin and 1g of glycerin in a small bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the blend and seal them immediately to ensure freshness and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with angina because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. The herb's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to cardiovascular disease and angina symptoms. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to increase nitric oxide production, leading to improved blood flow to the heart muscle. By increasing oxygen delivery to the heart, ginseng capsules may help reduce the frequency and severity of angina episodes.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective effects: Ginseng capsules have been shown to protect against cardiac damage and improve blood flow, reducing the risk of anginal attacks by improving overall cardiovascular health.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of ginseng capsules help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of angina by damaging blood vessels and reducing blood flow.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginseng capsules have been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the workload on the heart, thereby alleviating symptoms of angina.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help alleviate angina symptoms by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and inhibiting platelet aggregation, which helps to prevent blood clots from forming in the arteries.

Glycosides: Specifically, the ginsenoside Rg1, a type of glycoside, is known to help with angina by promoting angiogenesis (formation of new blood vessels), improving myocardial function, and reducing oxidative stress.

Furostanol saponins: Furostanol saponins, such as ginsenoside Rb1, also contribute to the anti-angina effects of Panax ginseng by inhibiting platelet aggregation, reducing inflammation, and scavenging free radicals, which helps to prevent oxidative damage to blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ginsenosides, which are believed to help alleviate angina symptoms by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Also contain ginsenosides, contributing to their potential benefits in managing angina through vasodilation and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: May be used due to their ginsenoside content, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood circulation to alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried panax ginseng root and sift it to remove impurities.
Grind the sifted panax ginseng root into a fine powder using a grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the ground panax ginseng powder with 250 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin mixture and insert the capsules into the machine.
Store the herbal panax ginseng capsules in an airtight container for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with angina because they possess adaptogenic properties that help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of anginal episodes. The herb's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects also contribute to its cardioprotective benefits, improving blood flow and reducing oxidative damage to the heart muscle. Additionally, ashwagandha has been shown to improve exercise tolerance and increase nitric oxide levels, which can help alleviate angina symptoms by reducing the frequency and severity of chest pain episodes.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety) Properties: Ashwagandha capsules help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common exacerbating factors for angina, allowing patients to manage their condition more effectively.

Cardioprotective Compounds: Ashwagandha contains bioactive compounds that have been shown to protect the heart from damage caused by oxidative stress, inflammation, and other cardiovascular risk factors, which can contribute to angina development or worsening.
Vasodilatory Effects: Ashwagandha's active compounds may help dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the heart and reducing symptoms of angina, such as chest pain or tightness.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to angina.
Withaferin A: As a bioactive alkaloid, Withaferin A may help protect against cardiac damage and improve cardiac function by regulating cell signaling pathways and reducing oxidative stress.

Ashwagandhanso A and B: These withanolides may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system and alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are primarily used due to their high content of bioactive compounds such as withanolides, which are believed to contribute to the potential therapeutic benefits for angina.
Leaves: The leaves contain withanolides, which are similar to those found in the roots and may help alleviate symptoms of angina.
Barks: The bark may be used due to its ability to contribute to a potential synergistic effect when combined with other parts of the plant, enhancing its overall efficacy for angina treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried withania somnifera roots from a trusted supplier or grown locally.
Clean the dried roots thoroughly by washing them in a mesh strainer under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Grind the cleaned withania somnifera roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the ground withania somnifera powder with 250 milligrams of a natural filler such as silicon dioxide.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixed powder using a powder filling machine or spoon and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with angina because they can help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart. The herb valerian root has a natural sedative effect that can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, both common triggers for anginal episodes. Additionally, valerian has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal valerian capsules may provide natural relief from angina symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cardiovascular relaxation: Valerian's ability to relax cardiovascular tissue helps reduce blood pressure, improving blood flow and oxygen supply to the heart, thereby alleviating symptoms of angina.

Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage heart tissue and contribute to angina, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system.
Calcium channel blocking activity: Valerian's calcium channel-blocking effect helps regulate blood vessel tone, which can improve coronary blood flow and reduce the frequency of anginal episodes.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene helps alleviate angina by reducing anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate heart conditions.
Valeranone: This valerenic acid derivative has sedative and antispasmodic properties, which can help calm the heart and reduce angina symptoms.
Valerenal: This sesquiterpene also possesses sedative and antispasmodic effects, contributing to its potential in reducing angina symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing heart rate.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are widely used due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which have been shown to have a sedative effect and help alleviate angina symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis also contain valerianine, a compound that has been found to have a positive impact on cardiovascular health and may help reduce the frequency of angina attacks.

Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used to treat various cardiovascular issues, including angina, due to their ability to relax the muscles and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the strain on the heart and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried root of valeriana officinalis from a trusted supplier or cultivate it yourself.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powder with 100-200 grams of gelatin and 50-100 grams of glycerin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal powder mixture using a powder filling machine.
Label and store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bayberry capsules helps with angina because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. Berberine also helps to lower triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels, which are often elevated in individuals with angina. Additionally, bayberry's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage, further contributing to its cardioprotective effects. By addressing these underlying factors, berberine-rich bayberry capsules can provide relief from the symptoms of angina and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic properties: Bayberry's cardiotonic properties help to strengthen and tone the heart muscle, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Bayberry's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and coronary arteries, which can contribute to angina pain by narrowing or blocking blood flow to the heart.
Antioxidant properties: Bayberry's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage the cardiovascular system and exacerbate angina symptoms, such as chest pain and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bayberry roots contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making them effective in relieving anginal symptoms.
Buds: Bayberry buds have antioxidant properties that help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of myrica pensylvanica in the morning for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves in shade for at least 2 hours before processing.
Mix 20 grams of dried myrica pensylvanica leaves with 10 grams of honey in a clean glass jar.
Let the mixture steep for 30 minutes before filtering it through a cheesecloth to extract the juice.
Fill empty capsules with 500 mg of the filtered juice and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with angina because they contain the plant's flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood flow to the heart by relaxing blood vessels. This increased blood flow can help to reduce the frequency and severity of anginal episodes by providing more oxygen-rich blood to the heart muscle. Additionally, yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its benefits in reducing symptoms of angina by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Yarrow's cardiac glycosides help to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks by increasing coronary blood flow.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in yarrow capsules have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to improved heart health and reduced risk of angina.

Saponins: Yarrow's saponins are known to exhibit vasodilatory effects, meaning they help to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing blood pressure, which can help alleviate anginal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots are used due to their ability to dilate blood vessels, which can help relieve anginal symptoms by improving blood flow to the heart.
Barks: The bark of the yarrow plant is used because it contains compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, making it effective in treating angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for preparation.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by spreading it on paper towels overnight to remove excess moisture.
Grind 30 grams of the cleaned plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered achillea millefolium with 2 grams of gelatin in a small bowl for 10 minutes to create a uniform mixture.
Fill #0 capsules with the herbal mixture, storing them in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with angina because they contain powerful antioxidants and flavonoids that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. The anthocyanins present in blueberries have been shown to relax the muscles in the coronary arteries, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing the frequency and severity of anginal episodes. This natural remedy can provide relief from chest pain and discomfort associated with angina, allowing individuals to resume their daily activities with improved quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to angina symptoms by improving blood flow to the heart muscle.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in blueberries have been shown to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing the risk of angina attacks.

Polyphenols: Polyphenols in blueberry capsules may help reduce platelet aggregation (clumping) and improve fibrinolysis (breakdown of blood clots), both of which can contribute to reduced blood flow to the heart, thereby alleviating angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in patients with angina.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce chest pain and discomfort associated with angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries from a trusted source and rinse thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the berries using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or until completely dry and brittle.
Grind the dried berries into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered vaccinium myrtillus with 500mg of gelatin in a small bowl and fill capsules according to manufacturer's instructions for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with angina because they promote a healthy cardiovascular system by improving blood flow to the heart. The capsule's active compounds, such as ferulic acid and hesperidin, help to relax blood vessels, reducing resistance to blood flow and increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle. Additionally, dong quai has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further alleviating symptoms of angina. By supporting cardiovascular health, dong quai capsules can provide relief from chest pain and discomfort associated with angina.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of angina.

Cardiovascular protection: The flavonoids and polysaccharides present in Dong Quai have been shown to improve cardiovascular function by reducing platelet aggregation, inhibiting platelet activation, and preventing vascular smooth muscle cell proliferation, all of which can help alleviate anginal symptoms.

Vasodilation and improved blood flow: Dong Quai's ability to dilate blood vessels and increase blood flow can help reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks by improving oxygen delivery to the heart muscle.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like ferulic acid, which helps to dilate blood vessels and reduce inflammation, alleviating anginal symptoms.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that help to improve cardiovascular function, reduce platelet aggregation, and dilate coronary arteries, thereby preventing angina attacks.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality dried angelica sinensis root powder of 500mg per serving.
Mix the powder with 1000mg of magnesium stearate as lubricant and anti-caking agent.
Fill small-sized #00 gelatin capsules with the mixture at a rate of 10 minutes per hundred capsules.
Seal each capsule using an automated capsule filling machine or manual twisting method for 15 minutes.
Store the prepared capsules in airtight containers at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with angina because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. The flavonoids and glycosides present in maypop capsules have been shown to improve blood flow to the heart muscle, reducing the risk of anginal episodes. By relaxing the blood vessels and improving circulation, maypop capsules may help alleviate symptoms of angina, such as chest pain and shortness of breath, providing a natural and holistic approach to managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: These compounds, such as digoxin, can help to strengthen the heart's contractions and reduce symptoms of angina by increasing the heart's pumping efficiency.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids like quercetin have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to anginal episodes.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids such as ellagic acid may help to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, both of which can alleviate symptoms of angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making it an effective treatment for angina.
Barks: The barks of the maypop plant are rich in tannins, which have been traditionally used to help reduce symptoms of angina by improving cardiac function and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried passiflora incarnata flowers from a reputable supplier.
Measure out 500 grams of the dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 250 grams of the ground passiflora incarnata with 100 grams of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Add 50 grams of water to the mixture and stir for 10 minutes to form a uniform paste.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the passiflora incarnata-gelatin mixture and seal the caps tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper capsules helps with angina because they contain piperine, a bioactive compound that increases blood flow to the heart. Piperine enhances the bioavailability of nitric oxide, a natural vasodilator, which relaxes blood vessels and improves blood flow to the heart. This increased blood flow reduces symptoms of angina by reducing cardiac workload and increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle. Additionally, piperine's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further alleviating angina symptoms. By improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, black pepper capsules provide a natural and effective adjunct therapy for managing angina.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which can contribute to angina.

Cineole: Cineole, another active component of black pepper, exhibits vasodilatory effects, meaning it helps relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can alleviate symptoms of angina by reducing the workload on the heart.
Piperyline: Piperyline, a breakdown product of piperine, has been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, which can help prevent the formation of blood clots that can contribute to anginal episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the black pepper plant are rich in bioactive compounds, such as piperine and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing anginal symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the black pepper plant contain a significant amount of piperine, a compound that has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, both of which can help alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried black pepper berries piper nigrum, 100g of tapioca starch, and 20 empty gelatin capsules.
Grind the dried black pepper berries into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 30% of the powdered black pepper with 70% of tapioca starch in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill each capsule shell with approximately 200mg of the blended mixture, using a small spoon or scoop to fill the capsules within 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, ensuring consistent potency and quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with angina because they contain isoflavones, which have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation. This increased blood flow helps reduce chest pain and discomfort associated with angina. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of red clover may help to relax blood vessel walls, further reducing symptoms. By addressing the underlying causes of angina, red clover capsules can provide natural relief from this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover capsules contain isoflavones, which have been shown to help improve blood flow and lower cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of heart disease and angina attacks.

Saponins: Saponins in red clover have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, contributing to improved cardiovascular health and reduced angina symptoms.
Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens in red clover capsules may help alleviate menopausal symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats, which can contribute to angina attacks in women.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of red clover contain flavonoids and isoflavones, which have been shown to help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, making them effective in alleviating angina symptoms.
Flowers: Red clover flowers are rich in saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, both of which are beneficial for managing angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried trifolium pratense flowers from a reputable supplier or grow your own.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for approximately 10 minutes.
Mix 2g of the powdered trifolium pratense with 98g of capsule filler to create a uniform blend taking about 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal blend and seal immediately to maintain potency within 30 seconds per capsule.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with angina because they have a positive impact on blood circulation, reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The diuretic properties of dandelion root extract help to increase blood flow to the heart muscle, thereby reducing symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath. Additionally, the antioxidant compounds in dandelion capsules help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to the cardiovascular system, further supporting overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Cardioactive compounds: Dandelion capsules contain cardioactive compounds such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, which have been shown to exhibit anti-ischemic and anti-arrhythmic effects, helping to alleviate the symptoms of angina by reducing the frequency and severity of cardiac spasms.

Vasodilatory properties: Dandelion contains flavonoids and terpenoids that help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart muscle and reducing the workload on the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating anginal symptoms such as chest pain and shortness of breath.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion capsules possess antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, a known contributor to the development and progression of cardiovascular disease and angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, which helps to dilate blood vessels and reduce inflammation associated with angina.
Buds: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to improve circulation and reduce platelet aggregation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly dry the harvested leaves using paper towels or air circulation for 24 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of powdered taraxacum officinale with 1000 milliliters of distilled water to create a decoction that steeps for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting decoction mixture and store in an airtight container at room temperature.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to angina that are treated with herbal capsules are conditions such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and chronic heart failure. Herbal capsules like Ginkgo biloba, Garlic, Hawthorn, and Ashwagandha have been traditionally used to improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and lower cholesterol levels, helping to alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, herbs like Turmeric, Ginger, and Fo-ti may help to reduce oxidative stress and improve overall cardiovascular health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with angina is a blend of Hawthorn, Turmeric, and Ginkgo Biloba. Hawthorn helps to improve cardiovascular function and reduce inflammation, while Turmeric's curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing pain and discomfort. Ginkgo Biloba enhances blood flow to the heart, reducing oxygen demand and alleviating angina symptoms. This combination promotes cardiovascular health, reduces inflammation, and improves overall heart function, making it a natural and effective remedy for angina sufferers. Consult a healthcare professional before taking any supplements.
